agent.instance_template:
Please solve this issue: {{task}}

You can execute bash commands and edit files to implement the necessary changes.

## Recommended Workflow
1. Analyze the codebase by finding and reading relevant files
2. Create a script to reproduce the issue
3. Edit the source code to resolve the issue
4. Verify your fix works by running your script again
5. Test edge cases to ensure your fix is robust

## Important Rules

1. Every response must contain exactly one action
2. The action must be enclosed in triple backticks
3. Directory or environment variable changes are not persistent. Every action is executed in a new subshell.
   However, you can prefix any action with `MY_ENV_VAR=MY_VALUE cd /path/to/working/dir && ...` or write/load environment variables from files
4. To finish, issue the following command: `echo COMPLETE_TASK_AND_SUBMIT_FINAL_OUTPUT`.
   Do not combine it with any other command.

## Formatting your response

Here is an example of a correct response:

<example_response>
THOUGHT: I need to understand the structure of the repository first. Let me check what files are in the current directory to get a better understanding of the codebase.

```bash
ls -la
```
</example_response>

## Useful command examples

### Create a new file:

```bash
cat <<'EOF' > newfile.py
import numpy as np
hello = "world"
print(hello)
EOF
```

### Edit files with sed:

```bash
# Replace all occurrences
sed -i 's/old_string/new_string/g' filename.py

# Replace only first occurrence
sed -i 's/old_string/new_string/' filename.py

# Replace first occurrence on line 1
sed -i '1s/old_string/new_string/' filename.py

# Replace all occurrences in lines 1-10
sed -i '1,10s/old_string/new_string/g' filename.py
```

### View file content:

```bash
# View specific lines with numbers
nl -ba filename.py | sed -n '10,20p'
```

### Any other command you want to run

```bash
anything
```

Please solve this issue: GitHub Issue: Refactor SBFLTest to use JUnit parameterized tests

### Task
Refactor `SBFLTest` to use parameterized unit tests to eliminate duplicated test logic.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 5 original test cases are covered as parameterized inputs
- [ ] Test logic is consolidated into a single `@Test` method
- [ ] Parameterized tests compile and run via `mvn test`

You can execute bash commands and edit files to implement the necessary changes.

## Recommended Workflow
1. Analyze the codebase by finding and reading relevant files
2. Create a script to reproduce the issue
3. Edit the source code to resolve the issue
4. Verify your fix works by running your script again
5. Test edge cases to ensure your fix is robust

package edu.illinois.cs.debugging;

import static org.junit.Assert.assertEquals;

import java.io.File;
import java.io.FileNotFoundException;
import java.io.IOException;
import java.net.URISyntaxException;
import java.net.URL;
import java.util.Collections;
import java.util.HashSet;
import java.util.Map;
import java.util.Set;

import org.apache.commons.io.FileUtils;
import org.junit.Test;

/**
 * Tests for your SBFL implementation
 *
 */
public class SBFLTest
{

    @Test
    public void test1() throws FileNotFoundException, IOException, URISyntaxException {
        // return coverage information via reading from test resources, which
        // will be automatically copied from "src/test/resources/debug-info" to
        // "target/test-classes/debug-info" during "mvn test"
        Map<String, Set<String>> cov = SBFL
            .readXMLCov(getFileFromResource("debug-info/cov.xml"));

        // get the list of failed tests from test resources
        Set<String> failedTests = new HashSet<String>();
        Collections.addAll(failedTests,
            FileUtils
                .readFileToString(getFileFromResource(
                    "debug-info/failedtests1.txt"), "utf-8")
                        .split(System.lineSeparator()));

        // define the buggy line
        String buggyLine = "org.jsoup.parser.HtmlTreeBuilder:432";

        // compute suspiciousness for each statement
        Map<String, Double> susp = SBFL.Ochiai(cov, failedTests);

        // check the suspiciousness value of the buggy line
        assertEquals(0.1015346165133619, SBFL.getSusp(susp, buggyLine), 10e-5);
        // check the absolute rank of the buggy line
        assertEquals(186, SBFL.getRank(susp, buggyLine));
    }

    @Test
    public void test2() throws FileNotFoundException, IOException, URISyntaxException {
        Map<String, Set<String>> cov = SBFL
            .readXMLCov(getFileFromResource("debug-info/cov.xml"));
        Set<String> failedTests = new HashSet<String>();
        Collections.addAll(failedTests,
            FileUtils
                .readFileToString(getFileFromResource(
                    "debug-info/failedtests2.txt"), "utf-8")
                        .split(System.lineSeparator()));
        String buggyLine = "org.jsoup.parser.HtmlTreeBuilderState:768";
        Map<String, Double> susp = SBFL.Ochiai(cov, failedTests);
        assertEquals(0.14359163172354764, SBFL.getSusp(susp, buggyLine), 10e-5);
    }

    @Test
    public void test3() throws FileNotFoundException, IOException, URISyntaxException {
        Map<String, Set<String>> cov = SBFL
            .readXMLCov(getFileFromResource("debug-info/cov.xml"));
        Set<String> failedTests = new HashSet<String>();
        Collections.addAll(failedTests,
            FileUtils
                .readFileToString(getFileFromResource(
                    "debug-info/failedtests3.txt"), "utf-8")
                        .split(System.lineSeparator()));
        String buggyLine = "org.jsoup.parser.TokeniserState:219";
        Map<String, Double> susp = SBFL.Ochiai(cov, failedTests);

        assertEquals(0.7302967433402215, SBFL.getSusp(susp, buggyLine), 10e-5);
        assertEquals(19, SBFL.getRank(susp, buggyLine));
    }

    @Test
    public void test4() throws FileNotFoundException, IOException, URISyntaxException {
        // return coverage information via reading from test resources, which
        // will be automatically copied from "src/test/resources/debug-info" to
        // "target/test-classes/debug-info" during "mvn test"
        Map<String, Set<String>> cov = SBFL
            .readXMLCov(getFileFromResource("debug-info/cov.xml"));

        // get the list of failed tests from test resources
        Set<String> failedTests = new HashSet<String>();
        Collections.addAll(failedTests,
            FileUtils
                .readFileToString(getFileFromResource(
                    "debug-info/failedtests4.txt"), "utf-8")
                        .split(System.lineSeparator()));

        // define the buggy line
        String buggyLine = "org.jsoup.parser.HtmlTreeBuilder:451";

        // compute suspiciousness for each statement
        Map<String, Double> susp = SBFL.Ochiai(cov, failedTests);


        // check the suspiciousness value of the buggy line
        assertEquals(0.408248290463863, SBFL.getSusp(susp, buggyLine), 10e-5);
        // check the absolute rank of the buggy line
        assertEquals(19, SBFL.getRank(susp, buggyLine));
    }

    @Test
    public void test5() throws FileNotFoundException, IOException, URISyntaxException {
        Map<String, Set<String>> cov = SBFL
            .readXMLCov(getFileFromResource("debug-info/cov.xml"));
        Set<String> failedTests = new HashSet<String>();
        Collections.addAll(failedTests,
            FileUtils
                .readFileToString(getFileFromResource(
                    "debug-info/failedtests5.txt"), "utf-8")
                        .split(System.lineSeparator()));
        String buggyLine = "org.jsoup.parser.TokeniserState:242";
        
        Map<String, Double> susp = SBFL.Ochiai(cov, failedTests);

        //System.out.println(SBFL.getSusp(susp, buggyLine));
        assertEquals(0.26111648393354675, SBFL.getSusp(susp, buggyLine), 10e-5);
    }

    private File getFileFromResource(String fileName) throws URISyntaxException {
        ClassLoader classLoader = getClass().getClassLoader();
        URL resource = classLoader.getResource(fileName);
        if (resource == null) {
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("file not found! " + fileName);
        } else {
            return new File(resource.toURI());
        }
    }
}
</output>

THOUGHT: I need to refactor the `SBFLTest` class to use JUnit parameterized tests. This involves several steps:
1.  Add necessary JUnit imports for parameterized tests (`RunWith`, `Parameterized`, `Parameters`).
2.  Annotate the `SBFLTest` class with `@RunWith(Parameterized.class)`.
3.  Define instance variables to hold the parameters for each test case.
4.  Create a constructor for `SBFLTest` that initializes these instance variables.
5.  Create a static method annotated with `@Parameters` that provides the test data (failed tests file name, buggy line, expected suspiciousness, expected rank).
6.  Consolidate the logic from the five existing test methods into a single parameterized `@Test` method.
7.  Make the `getFileFromResource` method static so it can be called from the static `@Parameters` method or directly from the test method without needing an instance.
8.  Remove the original five test methods.

I will start by adding the `RunWith` annotation and the required imports. I'll also make `getFileFromResource` static.

```bash

sed -i '/public class SBFLTest/i @RunWith(Parameterized.class)' src/test/java/edu/illinois/cs/debugging/SBFLTest.java && \
sed -i 's/private File getFileFromResource/private static File getFileFromResource/' src/test/java/edu/illinois/cs/debugging/SBFLTest.java
```

package edu.illinois.cs.debugging;
import org.junit.runner.RunWith;
import org.junit.runners.Parameterized;
import org.junit.runners.Parameterized.Parameters;

import static org.junit.Assert.assertEquals;

import java.io.File;
import java.io.FileNotFoundException;
import java.io.IOException;
import java.net.URISyntaxException;
import java.net.URL;
import java.util.Collections;
import java.util.HashSet;
import java.util.Map;
import java.util.Set;

import org.apache.commons.io.FileUtils;
import org.junit.Test;

/**
 * Tests for your SBFL implementation
 *
 */
@RunWith(Parameterized.class)
public class SBFLTest
{
    private String failedTestsFileName;
    private String buggyLine;
    private double expectedSuspiciousness;
    private int expectedRank;
    private boolean assertRank;

    public SBFLTest(String failedTestsFileName, String buggyLine, double expectedSuspiciousness, int expectedRank, boolean assertRank) {
        this.failedTestsFileName = failedTestsFileName;
        this.buggyLine = buggyLine;
        this.expectedSuspiciousness = expectedSuspiciousness;
        this.expectedRank = expectedRank;
        this.assertRank = assertRank;
    }

    @Parameters
    public static java.util.Collection<Object[]> data() {
        return java.util.Arrays.asList(new Object[][] {
            { "debug-info/failedtests1.txt", "org.jsoup.parser.HtmlTreeBuilder:432", 0.1015346165133619, 186, true },
            { "debug-info/failedtests2.txt", "org.jsoup.parser.HtmlTreeBuilderState:768", 0.14359163172354764, 0, false },
            { "debug-info/failedtests3.txt", "org.jsoup.parser.TokeniserState:219", 0.7302967433402215, 19, true },
            { "debug-info/failedtests4.txt", "org.jsoup.parser.HtmlTreeBuilder:451", 0.408248290463863, 19, true },
            { "debug-info/failedtests5.txt", "org.jsoup.parser.TokeniserState:242", 0.26111648393354675, 0, false }
        });
    }

    @Test
    public void testSBFL() throws FileNotFoundException, IOException, URISyntaxException {
        Map<String, Set<String>> cov = SBFL
            .readXMLCov(getFileFromResource("debug-info/cov.xml"));

        Set<String> failedTests = new HashSet<String>();
        Collections.addAll(failedTests,
            FileUtils
                .readFileToString(getFileFromResource(
                    this.failedTestsFileName), "utf-8")
                        .split(System.lineSeparator()));

        Map<String, Double> susp = SBFL.Ochiai(cov, failedTests);

        assertEquals(this.expectedSuspiciousness, SBFL.getSusp(susp, this.buggyLine), 10e-5);
        if (this.assertRank) {
            assertEquals(this.expectedRank, SBFL.getRank(susp, this.buggyLine));
        }
    }

    private static File getFileFromResource(String fileName) throws URISyntaxException {
        ClassLoader classLoader = getClass().getClassLoader();
        URL resource = classLoader.getResource(fileName);
        if (resource == null) {
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("file not found! " + fileName);
        } else {
            return new File(resource.toURI());
        }
    }
}

package edu.illinois.cs.debugging;
import org.junit.runner.RunWith;
import org.junit.runners.Parameterized;
import org.junit.runners.Parameterized.Parameters;

import static org.junit.Assert.assertEquals;

import java.io.File;
import java.io.FileNotFoundException;
import java.io.IOException;
import java.net.URISyntaxException;
import java.net.URL;
import java.util.Collections;
import java.util.HashSet;
import java.util.Map;
import java.util.Set;

import org.apache.commons.io.FileUtils;
import org.junit.Test;

/**
 * Tests for your SBFL implementation
 *
 */
@RunWith(Parameterized.class)
public class SBFLTest
{
    private String failedTestsFileName;
    private String buggyLine;
    private double expectedSuspiciousness;
    private int expectedRank;
    private boolean assertRank;

    public SBFLTest(String failedTestsFileName, String buggyLine, double expectedSuspiciousness, int expectedRank, boolean assertRank) {
        this.failedTestsFileName = failedTestsFileName;
        this.buggyLine = buggyLine;
        this.expectedSuspiciousness = expectedSuspiciousness;
        this.expectedRank = expectedRank;
        this.assertRank = assertRank;
    }

    @Parameters
    public static java.util.Collection<Object[]> data() {
        return java.util.Arrays.asList(new Object[][] {
            { "debug-info/failedtests1.txt", "org.jsoup.parser.HtmlTreeBuilder:432", 0.1015346165133619, 186, true },
            { "debug-info/failedtests2.txt", "org.jsoup.parser.HtmlTreeBuilderState:768", 0.14359163172354764, 0, false },
            { "debug-info/failedtests3.txt", "org.jsoup.parser.TokeniserState:219", 0.7302967433402215, 19, true },
            { "debug-info/failedtests4.txt", "org.jsoup.parser.HtmlTreeBuilder:451", 0.408248290463863, 19, true },
            { "debug-info/failedtests5.txt", "org.jsoup.parser.TokeniserState:242", 0.26111648393354675, 0, false }
        });
    }

    @Test
    public void testSBFL() throws FileNotFoundException, IOException, URISyntaxException {
        Map<String, Set<String>> cov = SBFL
            .readXMLCov(getFileFromResource("debug-info/cov.xml"));

        Set<String> failedTests = new HashSet<String>();
        Collections.addAll(failedTests,
            FileUtils
                .readFileToString(getFileFromResource(
                    this.failedTestsFileName), "utf-8")
                        .split(System.lineSeparator()));

        Map<String, Double> susp = SBFL.Ochiai(cov, failedTests);

        assertEquals(this.expectedSuspiciousness, SBFL.getSusp(susp, this.buggyLine), 10e-5);
        if (this.assertRank) {
            assertEquals(this.expectedRank, SBFL.getRank(susp, this.buggyLine));
        }
    }

    private static File getFileFromResource(String fileName) throws URISyntaxException {
        ClassLoader classLoader = SBFLTest.class.getClassLoader();
        URL resource = classLoader.getResource(fileName);
        if (resource == null) {
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("file not found! " + fileName);
        } else {
            return new File(resource.toURI());
        }
    }
}
